_metadata.title: ComplianceLint Interim Standard for EU AI Act Article 15: Accuracy, Robustness and Cybersecurity
_metadata.disclaimer: This is an interim standard developed by ComplianceLint based on available guidance, industry best practices, and relevant ISO standards. It is NOT an official standard. It will be superseded when CEN-CENELEC publishes the harmonized standard for Article 15 (expected Q4 2026).
basis: EU AI Act Recital 74 (legislative intent on accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity)
ISO/IEC 23894:2023 — Guidance on risk management for AI
ISO/IEC TR 24029-1:2021 — Assessment of the robustness of neural networks — Part 1: Overview
ISO/IEC TR 24029-2:2023 — Assessment of the robustness of neural networks — Part 2: Methodology
MITRE ATLAS — Adversarial Threat Landscape for AI Systems
_metadata.awaiting_standard: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for Art. 15 (expected Q4 2026)

requirements.title: Accuracy Metrics Definition and Tracking
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST define, track, and declare accuracy metrics appropriate for the system's intended purpose.
requirements.rationale: Art. 15(1) requires 'an appropriate level of accuracy' and Art. 15(2) requires 'accuracy metrics shall be declared in the accompanying instructions of use.' Without defined metrics, compliance cannot be assessed.
required_documentation: Metric values on validation and test sets
Performance breakdown by demographic groups (where applicable)

requirements.title: Testing Infrastructure
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST implement comprehensive testing including unit tests, integration tests, and model evaluation tests.
requirements.rationale: Art. 15(3) requires resilience to 'errors, faults or inconsistencies.' Testing is the primary mechanism for demonstrating and maintaining robustness.
acceptable_implementations: Unit tests for individual components (pytest, jest, JUnit)
Model evaluation tests (performance on test datasets)
End-to-end tests for critical user flows
requirements.minimum_coverage: Testing SHOULD cover all critical paths and edge cases. Coverage metrics SHOULD be tracked.

requirements.title: Input Validation and Sanitization
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST implement input validation and sanitization on all system entry points.
requirements.rationale: Art. 15(3) requires resilience to 'errors, faults or inconsistencies that may occur within the system or the environment in which the system operates.' Input validation is the first line of defense.
acceptable_implementations: Schema validation (pydantic, zod, JSON Schema, Protocol Buffers)
Image/audio/text format validation for ML inputs

requirements.title: Error Handling and Graceful Degradation
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST implement error handling with graceful degradation, including technically redundant solutions (back-up plans, fail-safe plans).
requirements.rationale: Art. 15(3) explicitly requires 'technically redundant solutions which may include back-up plans and fail-safe plans.'
acceptable_implementations: Graceful degradation (fallback to simpler model or rule-based system)
Circuit breaker pattern for external dependencies

requirements.title: Rate Limiting and Abuse Prevention
requirements.requirement: The provider SHOULD implement rate limiting on all public-facing endpoints to prevent abuse and denial-of-service attacks.
requirements.rationale: Art. 15(5) requires 'cybersecurity measures appropriate to the circumstances and the risks.' Rate limiting is a standard defense against service abuse.
acceptable_implementations: Per-client rate limiting (by IP, API key, or user ID)
Global rate limiting for system protection
Adaptive rate limiting based on threat signals

requirements.title: Authentication and Authorization
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST implement authentication and authorization controls on all system endpoints and administrative functions.
requirements.rationale: Art. 15(5) requires protection against 'attempts by unauthorised third parties.' Authentication verifies identity; authorization enforces access control.
acceptable_implementations: JWT (JSON Web Tokens) with proper validation

requirements.title: Dependency Vulnerability Scanning
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST integrate automated dependency vulnerability scanning into the development pipeline.
requirements.rationale: Art. 15(5) requires protection against exploitation of 'system vulnerabilities.' Third-party dependencies are a common attack vector.
acceptable_implementations: Dependabot or Renovate for automated updates
Trivy or Grype for container scanning
CodeQL or SonarQube for static analysis

requirements.title: Adversarial Testing
requirements.requirement: The provider SHOULD conduct adversarial testing appropriate to the system's risk profile and attack surface.
requirements.rationale: Art. 15(4) explicitly names adversarial ML attacks: 'data poisoning, model evasion, model inversion, and model inference.' Testing resilience against these is necessary.
acceptable_implementations: Red team exercises for AI systems
attack_types_to_test: Data poisoning: Can training data be manipulated?
Model evasion: Can adversarial inputs cause misclassification?
Model inversion: Can model outputs reveal training data?
Model inference: Can membership in training set be detected?

requirements.title: Redundancy and Failover
requirements.requirement: The provider SHOULD implement redundancy and failover mechanisms appropriate to the system's criticality.
requirements.rationale: Art. 15(3) requires 'technically redundant solutions which may include back-up plans and fail-safe plans.' For production systems, this translates to infrastructure redundancy.

scoring.compliant: R01 + R02 + R06 met, and at least 4 of R03-R05, R07-R09 met
scoring.partial: R01 or R02 met + at least 4 others met
scoring.non_compliant: Neither R01 nor R02 met, or fewer than 4 requirements met
